key_risks: STAR Market listing risks: companies listed on the STAR Market have characteristics of high R&D investment, high operational risk, unstable performance, and high delisting risk, exposing investors to significant market risk.

Technology and product iteration risk: the general robotics industry requires rapid and continuous technology and product updates; if the company fails to keep pace with technological advancements, it may lose its competitive advantage.

High R&D investment risk: the company operates in a technology-intensive, R&D-driven industry with large and ongoing R&D expenditures, which may not yield expected returns and could affect profitability.
Business performance instability risk: as a high-growth technology company at an early stage of commercialization, operating results may fluctuate significantly.

Industry competition risk: the humanoid robot and quadruped robot industries are highly competitive globally, and intensified competition could negatively impact the company's market share and margins.

Key personnel and talent retention risk: the company relies heavily on specialized talent in AI, motion control, and perception; failure to attract or retain key personnel could adversely affect operations.

Commercialization and market adoption risk: embodied intelligence and general-purpose robots are at an early stage of development; market acceptance and large-scale deployment may be slower than expected.
Supply chain and component risk: the company depends on external suppliers for certain key components; supply disruptions could affect production and delivery.

International market and geopolitical risk: the company has significant overseas sales and recognition; changes in international trade policies, export controls, or geopolitical tensions could adversely impact overseas business.
Delisting risk: as a STAR Market listed company, the company faces the inherent risk of delisting if it fails to meet continued listing requirements.
Early-stage industrialization risk: the robotics hardware and software technology are still at an early rapid-development stage, and the pace of industrialization may be uncertain.
Corporate governance risk: the company is transitioning to a publicly listed company governance structure; failure to effectively implement governance improvements could affect investor interests.

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project
projects.focus: R&D of embodied intelligence AI models, including reinforcement learning, high-precision control, and frontier AI technology for general-purpose robots

projects.name: Robot Body Research and Development Project
projects.focus: R&D of robot hardware bodies, including humanoid robots and quadruped robots, improving motion performance, robustness, and humanization level

projects.name: New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project
projects.focus: Development of new intelligent robot products, expanding product matrix including robot components such as joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, and perception sensors

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project
projects.focus: Construction of a scaled, intelligent manufacturing facility to enhance industrialization capability and production capacity for high-performance general-purpose robots
